text:
Once upon a time in the quiet town of Everwood, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. Every evening, he would glide silently through the leaves, plotting his grand migration south. His favorite pastime? Watching the children at the local playground play tag.

On this particular Sunday, Oliver noticed something unusual. A group of pigeons had formed a team. Each bird had its own special skill—some could fly farther, some faster. They worked closely, supporting each other during races. Oliver decided to join their team.

The next weekend was a competition. Oliver wasn’t just any old owl; he was charismatic. With his tales of adventurous travels, he inspired the pigeons to new heights. They trained hard, practising their climbs. Oliver became their coach. By the final race, they were a sight to behold—a flock of winged competitors ready to conquer.
